data.content:
### oauth2l --credentials Option

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Specifies an OAuth credential file (client ID or Service Account key) for the authentication flow. Falls back to GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/service_account.json --scope cloud-platform
```

```bash
$ export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS="~/service_account.json"
$ oauth2l fetch --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Access Token with Credentials File

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Fetches an OAuth 2.0 access token using a specified JSON credentials file. This command is useful when running in environments without pre-configured Google Cloud SDK or service account metadata.

```bash
oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/your_credentials.json --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l --json Option Example

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Shows how to use the --json option to specify a credential file (OAuth client ID or Service Account key) for initiating an OAuth flow.

```Shell
$ oauth2l fetch --json ~/service_account.json cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Command-Line Options Reference

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Provides a reference for common oauth2l command-line options, detailing their purpose and usage. This includes authentication types, credential management, scopes, and token manipulation.

```APIDOC
oauth2l COMMAND [OPTIONS]

COMMANDS:
  fetch: Fetches an OAuth access token.
  header: Sets headers for subsequent requests.

OPTIONS:
  --type <oauth|jwt|sso>
    Specifies the authentication flow type.
    - oauth: Uses 2-legged or 3-legged OAuth flow.
    - jwt: Generates a JWT token signed by a service account.
    - sso: Uses an external SSO CLI to fetch tokens.

  --credentials <path>
    Path to the service account key file or client credentials file.

  --scope <scope1,scope2,...>
    The scope(s) to authorize. Required for oauth and sso.
    Can be a comma-delimited list or a quoted space-delimited string.
    Example: "cloud-platform pubsub"

  --audience <url>
    The single audience for JWT or STS tokens. Required for jwt.

  --sts
    If true, exchanges the access token for an STS token.
    Can be used with --audience and --quota_project.
    Incompatible with --type jwt.

  --quota_project <project_id>
    The quota project for STS token claims.

  --email <email_address>
    The email associated with SSO or for domain-wide delegation.
    Required for sso.

  --ssocli <path>
    Path to a custom SSO CLI executable.

  --cache <path>
    Path to the token cache file. Empty string disables caching.
    Defaults to ~/.oauth2l.

  --refresh
    Attempt to refresh expired tokens from cache using refresh tokens.

  --impersonate-service-account <id_or_email>
    Impersonates a service account to get its token.
    Requires specific scopes (e.g., cloud-platform, iam).

Examples:
  Fetch token with OAuth flow:
    $ oauth2l fetch --type oauth --credentials ~/client.json --scope cloud-platform

  Fetch token with JWT and audience:
    $ oauth2l fetch --type jwt --credentials ~/sa.json --audience https://example.com/

  Fetch token with SSO and custom CLI:
    $ oauth2l header --type sso --ssocli /usr/local/bin/my_sso --email user@example.com --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with Email for SSO

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Specifies the email address associated with the SSO authentication type. The email can also be used for domain-wide delegation with service account credentials.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --type sso --email me@google.com --scope cloud-platform
```

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/service_account.json --scope cloud-platform --email user@google.com
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with JWT Flow

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Generates a JWT token signed by a service account private key. Requires either --audience or --scope to be specified. The --credentials option must point to a service account key file.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --type jwt --credentials ~/service_account.json --audience https://pubsub.googleapis.com/
```

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --type jwt --credentials ~/service_account.json --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l header Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Outputs an OAuth2 access token in the HTTP header format, suitable for use with tools like curl. It supports specifying scopes and credentials for authentication.

```bash
$ oauth2l header --scope cloud-platform
Authorization: Bearer ya29.zyxwvutsrqpnmolkjihgfedcba
```

```bash
$ curl -H "$(oauth2l header --scope pubsub)" https://pubsub.googleapis.com/v1/projects/my-project-id/topics
```

```bash
$ curl -H "$(oauth2l header --email user@example.com --credentials service_account_credentials.json --scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.labels)" https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/labels
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with Token Refresh

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Attempts to refresh an expired access token from the cache using a refresh token instead of re-authorizing the user. Requires valid credentials and a cached refresh token.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/client_credentials.json --scope cloud-platform --refresh
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l --sso and --sso_cli Option Examples

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Illustrates using external Single Sign-on (SSO) commands to fetch OAuth tokens. Demonstrates fetching headers with and without specifying the SSO CLI path.

```Shell
# Example SSO command outputting a token
$ sso me@example.com scope1 scope2

# Using oauth2l with SSO CLI
$ oauth2l header --sso me@example.com --sso_cli /usr/bin/sso cloud-platform
$ oauth2l header --sso me@google.com cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### Web Command Directory

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Installs OAuth2l-web packages to a specific directory. If this option is used, it must be provided again for future executions of the web command, such as stopping or restarting the web app.

```bash
$ oauth2l web --directory your/new/directory
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with Custom SSO CLI Path

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Specifies the file path to a custom SSO CLI executable for use with the 'sso' authentication type.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --type sso --ssocli /usr/bin/sso --email me@google.com --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Install oauth2l from Source

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Guides users on building and installing oauth2l from its GitHub repository for Linux or Mac systems with Go 1.10.3 or higher. It involves cloning the repository, making the binary, and optionally adding it to the system's PATH.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/google/oauth2l
cd oauth2l
make dev
# Optional: Add to PATH if not already done
# ~/go/bin/oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/your_credentials.json --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### Install oauth2l

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Instructions for installing the oauth2l package using pip. Includes common flags like --upgrade and --ignore-installed for specific OS versions.

```Shell
# Mac only. Install `pip` first.
$ sudo easy_install pip

# Install `oauth2l` under the OS, typically "/usr/local/bin".
$ pip install google-oauth2l --upgrade

# If you see an error on OS X El Capitan or up, please try
$ pip install google-oauth2l --upgrade --ignore-installed

# Install `oauth2l` under the current user, typically "~/.local/bin" (on Linux)
# and "~/Library/Python/2.7/bin" (on Mac).
$ pip install --user google-oauth2l
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l --help Option

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Prints help messages for the main oauth2l program or specific subcommands.

```bash
$ oauth2l --help
```

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --help
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l --type Option

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Specifies the authentication type, supporting 'oauth', 'jwt', or 'sso'. Defaults to 'oauth'.

--------------------------------

### oauth2l web Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Deploys and launches the OAuth2l Playground web application locally. Requires Docker and docker-compose. This feature is experimental.

```bash
$ oauth2l web
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with Service Account Impersonation

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Exchanges a fetched User access token for a Service Account access token using Google's IAM Service. Requires the --impersonate-service-account parameter (ID or email) and specific scopes like 'cloud-platform' or 'iam'.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --credentials ~/client_credentials.json --scope cloud-platform,pubsub --impersonate-service-account 113258942105700140798
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with SSO Flow

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Fetches an OAuth access token using an external Single Sign-on (SSO) CLI. Requires an email address and scope. A custom SSO CLI can be specified using the --ssocli option.

```bash
$ oauth2l header --type sso --email me@google.com --scope cloud-platform
```

```bash
$ oauth2l header --type sso --ssocli /usr/bin/sso --email me@google.com --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### Custom SSO CLI Interface

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Defines the expected interface for a custom SSO CLI tool used with oauth2l. The CLI should accept an email and one or more scopes as arguments.

```bash
$ /usr/bin/sso me@example.com scope1 scope2
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l reset Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Resets all locally cached OAuth2 tokens, typically stored in ~/.oauth2l.

```bash
$ oauth2l reset
```

--------------------------------

### Install oauth2l with Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Installs the oauth2l tool on macOS using the Homebrew package manager. This is a convenient way to get the latest version if available, though new releases may have propagation delays.

```bash
brew install oauth2l
```

--------------------------------

### Run oauth2l using Docker

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Executes oauth2l within an official Docker container to fetch an access token with the cloud-platform scope. This method allows using the tool without local installation, ensuring a consistent environment.

```sh
docker run -it gcr.io/oauth2l/oauth2l header cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l Fetch with OAuth Flow

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Fetches an OAuth access token using the 2-legged or 3-legged OAuth flow. Requires either a service account key for 2-legged flow or OAuth Client ID for 3-legged flow. The --scope parameter is mandatory.

```bash
$ oauth2l fetch --type oauth --credentials ~/client_credentials.json --scope cloud-platform
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l test Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Tests the validity of an OAuth2 token by setting the shell exit code. An exit code of 0 indicates a valid token, while 1 indicates an invalid token.

```APIDOC
oauth2l test <token>
  - Checks the validity of an OAuth2 token.
  - Sets the shell exit code: 0 for valid, 1 for invalid.
  - Useful for scripting and shell pipelines.
  - Parameters:
    - token: The OAuth2 token string to test.
  - Example Usage:
    $ oauth2l test ya29.zyxwvutsrqpnmolkjihgfedcba
    $ echo $?
    0

    $ oauth2l test ya29.justkiddingmadethisoneup
    $ echo $?
    1
```

--------------------------------

### New Build System Dockerfile

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/vendor/golang.org/x/sys/unix/README.md

The Dockerfile for the new build system is located in the `${GOOS}` directory. It specifies how to set up the container environment to checkout kernel and system library sources for generating Go files.

```dockerfile
${GOOS}/Dockerfile
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l test Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/README.md

Tests a token's validity by setting the exit code to 0 for valid tokens and 1 for invalid ones, useful for shell scripting.

```bash
$ oauth2l test --token ya29.zyxwvutsrqpnmolkjihgfedcba
0
$ echo $?
0
$ oauth2l test --token ya29.justkiddingmadethisoneup
1
$ echo $?
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l --jwt Option Example

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Demonstrates using the --jwt option with a service account key file to generate a JWT token signed by the service account's private key. Requires a JWT audience.

```Shell
oauth2l fetch --jwt --json ~/service_account.json https://pubsub.googleapis.com/google.pubsub.v1.Publisher
```

--------------------------------

### oauth2l reset Command

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/python/README.md

Resets all locally cached OAuth2 tokens. Cached tokens are stored in the file '~/.oauth2l.token'.

```APIDOC
oauth2l reset
  - Clears all previously cached OAuth2 tokens.
  - This action removes the contents of the token cache file '~/.oauth2l.token'.
  - Example Usage:
    $ oauth2l reset
```

--------------------------------

### Install Go Module: cloud.google.com/go/compute/metadata

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/vendor/cloud.google.com/go/compute/metadata/README.md

Installs the Google Cloud Compute metadata Go module using the go get command. This command fetches the latest version of the specified module and its dependencies, making it available for use in your Go projects.

```bash
go get cloud.google.com/go/compute/metadata
```

--------------------------------

### Build System Commands (mkall.sh)

Source: https://github.com/google/oauth2l/blob/master/vendor/golang.org/x/sys/unix/README.md

The mkall.sh script is used to generate Go files for the sys/unix package. It can be run with or without the -n flag to show the commands that will be executed. The behavior depends on whether the old or new build system is active.

```bash
mkall.sh
# Shows commands without executing them
```

```bash
mkall.sh -n
```
